metadata.name: Required Minimum Distribution Rules History
metadata.description: History of RMD starting ages, rule changes, and key policy milestones. Essential for retirement distribution planning and Roth conversion windows.
notes: RMDs apply to traditional IRAs, 401(k), 403(b), TSP, and most employer plans.
Roth IRAs: No RMDs for original owner (since SECURE 2.0, effective 2024). Inherited Roth IRAs still have RMDs.
Roth 401(k)/TSP: No RMDs starting 2024 (SECURE 2.0). Previously required RMDs like traditional accounts.
The window between retirement and RMD age is the prime Roth conversion opportunity.
Penalty for missed RMD: reduced from 50% to 25% (10% if corrected timely) by SECURE 2.0.

rmd_age_history.law: Tax Reform Act of 1986 / IRS regulations
rmd_age_history.notes: Original RMD age. Applied to all traditional retirement accounts.
First RMD age increase in over 30 years.
Second increase. Born 1951-1959: RMDs begin at 73.
Born 1960 or later: RMDs begin at 75. Longest Roth conversion window.

key_rule_changes.change: Simplified RMD calculation using Uniform Lifetime Table

key_rule_changes.law: Worker, Retiree, and Employer Recovery Act
key_rule_changes.change: RMD waiver for 2009 due to financial crisis
RMD waiver for 2020 due to COVID-19
RMDs resumed for 2021 following 2020 waiver. Normal distribution rules reinstated.
New Uniform Lifetime Table effective 2022 — reduced RMD amounts (longer life expectancy factors)
RMD age increased to 73. Penalty reduced to 25% (10% if corrected). Roth employer plan accounts exempted from RMDs starting 2024.
Roth 401(k)/403(b)/TSP no longer subject to RMDs for original owner.

Finalized inherited IRA 10-year rule: non-eligible designated beneficiaries must take annual RMDs during the 10-year period when original owner had reached RMD age. Effective for distributions in 2025.
RMD age increases to 75 for those born 1960+.

rmd_penalty_history.notes: 50% excise tax on amount not distributed
Further reduced to 10% if corrected within 2 years

inherited_ira_rules.pre_secure_act: Stretch IRA: beneficiaries could take RMDs over their own life expectancy
inherited_ira_rules.post_secure_act_2020: Most non-spouse beneficiaries must empty inherited IRA within 10 years (no annual RMD required, just 10-year liquidation)
exceptions_to_10_year: Minor children (until age of majority, then 10-year clock starts)
Beneficiaries not more than 10 years younger than the deceased
inherited_ira_rules.irs_2024_final_rules: Annual RMDs ARE required during the 10-year window if the original owner had already begun RMDs. Final regulations effective 2025.
